title: festuca novae-zelandiae
intro: Festuca novae-zelandiae, also known as New Zealand fescue, is a type of grass native to New Zealand, with tall stems and blue-green foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, with compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids showing potential in reducing inflammation and improving antioxidant activity. The health benefits of Festuca novae-zelandiae include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to the prevention of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. Festuca novae-zelandiae is typically prepared as an infusion or tea, with some herbal preparations also using the plant's leaves and stems in combination with other botanicals to enhance its medicinal properties.
